'''Lusa''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Chironian]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Chiron (planet)|Chiron]]. She joined the [[New Jedi Order]] after deserting the [[Diversity Alliance]], and was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[voxyn]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].

==Biography==
===Captivity===
{{Quote|Take me. Take me instead, don't take Lusa away, don't cut off her horns|Jaina Solo, pleading with Hethrir to not take Lusa away|<ref name="The Crystal Star">''[[The Crystal Star]]''</ref>}}
As a child, Lusa was abducted by the [[Empire Reborn]] movement and interned on [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]]'s [[worldcraft]], where she attended the "school" there which propagated Empire Reborn propaganda. She was bullied by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] captives there including the [[Helpers|Helper]] [[Vram]] who made [[Speciesism|speciesist]] comments about cutting off her horns. In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], she met the two children of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]], who had also been abducted.

After failing a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] test where Hethrir deliberately made it impossible for non-Humans to demonstrate any [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-ability by dampening their [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]], she was consigned to [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] despite the protests of Jaina. Later, when the [[Solo family/Legends|Solos]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] [[Confrontation on Crseih Station|confronted]] Hethrir and his Empire Reborn at [[Crseih Station]], Lusa managed to break free of her captors and came to the aid of the Solo twins and Chewbacca by attacking several [[Proctor (Empire Reborn)|Proctors]] and [[Empire Youth]].

With the defeat of the Empire Reborn, she was freed and returned to her homeworld and family though the experience gave her a [[Anti-Humanism|prejudice against Humans]] which she would harbor for many years to come. Even Lusa's family was not able to understand her pain and she had a difficult time making friends or trusting anyone until she met the [[Diversity Alliance]].

===Diversity Alliance===
{{Quote|I had never stopped to think exactly what I was willing to do for the cause I believed in. Was I willing to kill? And if so, what must the victim's crime be to deserve death? Should I judge each one, or could I trust my leader to judge them for me?|Lusa commenting on her experiences|<ref name="Young Jedi Knights: Delusions of Grandeur">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Delusions of Grandeur]]''</ref>}}
Around [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], she met a few members of the alien-rights group, the Diversity Alliance. They had all experienced some form of [[humanocentrism]] expressed towards them by Humans—particularly Imperials—and also shared her hatred. Believing that they were trying to make [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] a better place for non-Human species and having found a sense of belonging, she joined them and was further indoctrinated by their propaganda which blamed Humans for all the galaxy's wrongs.

As she rose through the ranks of the Diversity Alliance, the leader [[Nolaa Tarkona]] began sending her on covert operations which included saving the lives of aliens, freeing slaves and overthrowing leaders who opposed the Alliance. Around [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], she was sent on a mission to sabotage the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputers]] of a Human-crewed geological survey ship which had accidentally destroyed a forest on the planet [[Kaisa]] and inadvertently wiped out the insectoid [[Buro]] people. Her job was to sabotage the vessel's navicomputer so that it would be lost when they emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].

However, following the mission, she felt a pang of guilt when she saw that one of the geologists had a teenage daughter. With her eyes opened to reality and her indoctrination washed away, Lusa went up to them as they were loading the sabotaged navicomputer aboard and admitted her guilt in sabotaging in it and placed herself at their mercy. Instead of receiving vengeance, the geologists forgave her and had the device repaired.

Following that, the geologists offered to transport her wherever she wanted. She chose to go to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] because she wanted to contact either Leia Organa Solo who had been re-elected the Chief of State or [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] Luke Skywalker, who now headed the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and inform them about the true identity of the Diversity Alliance which had now categorized her as a traitor to their cause. It was from there that she went to Yavin 4.

There, at Skywalker's Jedi academy, she befriended him as well as the [[Solo twins]] and also formed a bond with [[Raynar Thul]]. She brought attention to the fact that the Diversity Alliance was not what it seemed, and she almost died when an undercover [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] Alliance member shot her with a tranquillizer while she was resting in a river. Luckily, Skywalker came to her rescue and apprehended the assassin.

Lusa accompanied a New Republic [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senate]] team exploring the claims made against the Diversity Alliance concerning the stockpiling of weapons for a war against Humans and the New Republic. With the defeat of the Alliance, she stayed at the academy to learn to focus her [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force healing/Legends|healing powers]] and become a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].

===Death===
During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Lusa was resting in a field on Chiron when a [[voxyn]] struck her down.
